result: I’d give every human mind the ability to hold contradictory truths without shattering—so we could see the full spectrum of color in every person and idea without having to bleach or flatten them to feel safe. A mind that can cradle both “I am right” and “I might be wrong” at the same time would unwind every other knot we keep trying to cut: war, fanaticism, waste, loneliness. The world wouldn’t become a monotone peace; it would become polyphonic—thousands of clashing, cooperating notes kept in earshot instead of muted. That single rewiring—immunity to the reflex that equates difference with threat—would let every other change we dream of grow without being strangled in its cradle.

The world wouldn’t become a monotone peace; it would become polyphonic—thousands of clashing, cooperating notes kept in earshot instead of muted. That single rewiring—immunity to the reflex that equates difference with threat—would let every other change we dream of grow without being strangled in its cradle.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
